title: IDID Chart Capability
description: A library-neutral, declarative description of what a chart is good at, so an agent or a heuristic engine can rank it against a dataset and an intent WITHOUT executing any rendering library. This is the serializable subset of a chart's capability — the imperative gates (fits, buildProps, scoring functions) a host library may attach are implementation details and are intentionally NOT part of the portable spec. See ./README.md for the field-status convention (x-idid-status).

specVersion.description: The IDID portability spec version this descriptor targets.

component.description: Stable name of the chart this descriptor is about (e.g. "BarChart", "SankeyDiagram"). The portable identity of the chart; how a host resolves it to a renderer is a binding concern.

family.description: Coarse chart family. Open string — recognized values are listed in examples but a host may use its own taxonomy.

importPath.description: Where the implementing library exposes the chart (e.g. "semiotic/ordinal"). Advisory; a binding may ignore it.

rubric.description: Audience-independent base rubric, before any audience override or variant delta.

intentScores.description: Per-intent suitability, 0–5. Missing intents default to 0. Keys are intent ids — the 13 built-in ids are in $defs/intentId, and hosts may register custom intents (open string).

variants.description: Settings that meaningfully change what the chart is good for (e.g. a streamgraph variant of a stacked area). Deltas are additive against the base intentScores/rubric and clamped to range by the consumer.

caveats.description: Static, audience-independent caveats this chart always carries (e.g. "log scale skipped for negative values"). The portable form of a host's dynamic caveat function: the host may emit additional data-dependent caveats at runtime.

mobile.description: Phone-specific design contract for mobile audits and responsive adapters. This lets custom charts and interoperability-generated charts declare how they preserve the task at small widths.

tags.description: Free-form classification tags consumers can filter on.

rubric.title: Chart Rubric
rubric.description: Three 1–5 axes. familiarity = how well-known the chart is to a general audience; accuracy = how faithfully it represents the data; precision = how readable individual values are.

key.description: Stable variant identifier within the capability.

rubricDeltas.description: Additive rubric deltas — usually small (e.g. smoothing trades precision for familiarity).

props.description: Implementation-specific props the host merges to realize this variant. Opaque to the spec; a non-Semiotic host may ignore it.

strategy.description: Overall mobile design strategy, e.g. responsive, small-multiples, summary-cards, direct-labels, fixed-artboard.

intentId.title: Intent
intentId.description: Communicative intent the chart is being ranked for. The 13 built-in ids are below; hosts may register additional intents, so this is an open string in practice.
